in_session_step_3_5_iteration_detail: Step 3.5 first-run produced 9/10 keyword-content-alignment PASS with the 'hemorrhage' keyword for GT1 not surfacing as a literal substring in the initial GT1.content draft because the draft used the hyphenated compound 'subarachnoid-blood' in place of the canonical medical phrase. Applied a single in-session Step 3.5 single-token-iteration substitution of 'showing diffuse subarachnoid-blood across the basal-cisterns' -> 'showing diffuse subarachnoid hemorrhage SAH across the basal-cisterns' (two occurrences via replace_all=True: GT1.content + intent_1) inserting the canonical phrase 'subarachnoid hemorrhage' plus the SAH acronym - mirrors M11a-extension D-C2.attempt2.C QDRO-acronym-insertion + M11a-extension-extension D-C3.attempt1.C in-session Step 3.5 single-token iteration precedent (single-token fix vs spelled-out form, NOT counted as rejection-and-retry per M11a-extension D-C3.attempt1.C precedent). Step 3.5 re-run PASSed 10/10 after the substitution.
first_run_step_6_overlap_breakdown_note: Step 6 first-run produced 14 inadvertent 8-gram cross-trace overlaps when the full draft was sweep-checked AFTER applying the D3-opener pre-write paraphrase (caught at the candidate pre-check on the 'mailing-list email landed on the household-subscriber inbox announcing the Resend' opener 2-grams). Counting the D3-opener cluster as a 5th cluster (which would have surfaced at the file-level step-6 had I not pre-fixed it at the candidate-check sweep stage), the total step-6 first-run overlap count is 16 instances across 5 distinct convergence sources
